senses.definition: a chance or opportunity given to a person equally as others

examples.x: I believe everyone deserves a fair shake in life.
The company ensures that employees receive a fair shake in terms of promotions and opportunities.
She felt that she didn't get a fair shake in the previous job interview.
We will strive to provide every student with a fair shake in terms of educational opportunities.
The new policy aims to ensure a fair shake for all participants in the upcoming event.

senses.definition: to receive an unfortunate consequence or punishment that one truly deserves as a result of one's wrong behavior

examples.x: It serves him right for constantly interrupting others during meetings.
The consequences they are facing serve them right for cheating on the exam.
The accident served as a wake-up call, and it served him right for texting while driving.
She failed the project, and it served her right for not putting in the effort.
If he continues to ignore safety protocols, it will serve him right when he faces disciplinary action.
If they don't take the necessary precautions, it will serve them right if they end up getting injured.

senses.definition: done honestly, justly, or without disobeying any rules

examples.x: I always play by the rules and compete fair and square.
The negotiation process should be conducted fair and square, without any hidden agendas.
Despite fierce competition, she won the race fair and square.
They resolved the conflict by addressing the issues fair and square.
We will conduct the evaluation process fair and square to ensure unbiased results.
The company promises to distribute bonuses fair and square based on performance metrics.

word_list.word: [put] the saddle on the right horse
word_list.pronunciation: pˌʊt ðə sˈædəl ɑːnðə ɹˈaɪt hˈɔːɹs

senses.definition: to blame the right person or thing

examples.x: It is important to put the saddle on the right horse to ensure optimal performance and productivity.
She always ensures that the tasks are assigned appropriately, putting the saddle on the right horse.
They realized their mistake and acknowledged that they didn't put the saddle on the right horse for that project.
He learned from his past experiences and made sure to put the saddle on the right horse when delegating responsibilities.
In the upcoming project, we will carefully consider each team member's strengths to put the saddle on the right horse.

senses.definition: of the same level, status, or position, without any advantage or disadvantage for one person, thing, or group over the other

examples.x: We strive to ensure that all employees are treated on an equal footing, regardless of their background or position.
The organization operates on the principle of providing opportunities to everyone on an equal footing.
After the policy changes, the company finally started treating all employees on an equal footing.
In the previous administration, efforts were made to bring different communities on an equal footing.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡˈɪv ðə dˈɛvəl hɪz ɔːɹ hɜː dˈuː ɪn lˈɔː ɔːɹ

senses.definition: to acknowledge someone's positive qualities or actions, even if that person is generally unpleasant, evil, or disliked

examples.x: In the future, we will continue to give the devil his dues by recognizing the positive impact of individuals who have faced adversity.

senses.definition: in a transparent, honest, and lawful manner

examples.x: The company ensures that all financial transactions are conducted above board and in compliance with legal regulations.
In the upcoming merger, both companies have committed to conducting all negotiations and agreements in an above board fashion.
As we move forward, our aim is to establish an above board culture within the organization, promoting integrity and ethical behavior.
The company prides itself on offering a square deal to its employees, providing fair wages and equal opportunities for growth.
We believe in treating our customers with a square deal, ensuring transparency, and delivering high-quality products.
Despite the challenges faced, the team managed to negotiate a square deal that satisfied all parties involved.
The organization consistently provided a square deal to its clients, earning a reputation for fairness and integrity.
In the upcoming merger, both companies have committed to striking a square deal that benefits all stakeholders involved.

senses.definition: a situation in which one believes that an unfortunate event that has happened to someone is well deserved

examples.x: The villain's downfall in the story provides a perfect example of poetic justice, as their wicked actions catch up to them.
The protagonist's noble actions are consistently rewarded throughout the narrative, showcasing instances of poetic justice.
The film concluded with a satisfying sense of poetic justice, as the corrupt politician met his ultimate downfall.
In the novel, the deceitful character faced poetic justice when their web of lies unraveled, leading to their social isolation.
The upcoming play promises to deliver moments of poetic justice, where the protagonists triumph over adversity and the antagonists face their comeuppance.
